teaching_language: Italian (programme taught in Italian; English B2 is required as a competence, not as the language of instruction)
campus: Genoa (DICCA - Department of Civil, Chemical and Environmental Engineering)
admission_type: open access (free access, but admission requires meeting curricular requirements and, where applicable, verification of personal preparation)

academic_requirements.summary: Open-access (non-competitive) master's programme taught in Italian. Admission requires a recognised prior degree (three-year, specialised or master's, or pre-DM 509/1999 five-year), satisfaction of curricular CFU requirements, English B2 competence, and verification of personal preparation when the bachelor's grade is below the published threshold.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A three-year degree, specialised or master's degree pursuant to DM 509/1999 or DM 270/2004, or a five-year degree (prior to DM 509/1999), or equivalent qualifications.
academic_requirements.curricular_requirements: Direct curricular access for holders of a degree in Class L-7 Civil and Environmental Engineering. Otherwise the candidate must hold at least 36 CFU in the basic-training scientific-disciplinary sectors of Class L-7 Civil and Environmental Engineering, and at least 45 CFU in core-training activities in Civil Engineering and Environmental/Territorial Engineering disciplines.
academic_requirements.minimum_grade: The initial-preparation interview is waived for holders of a bachelor's degree with a final grade of at least 9/10 of the maximum, or at least 92/110, or corresponding to ECTS classification 'A'. [uncertain] No non-EU minimum-grade threshold is published because the programme is not offered through the UniGeApply English master's pre-evaluation route.
academic_requirements.personal_preparation_assessment: Candidates below the grade threshold sit a public interview assessing general preparation, in particular knowledge of the fundamentals of civil and environmental engineering, with consideration of the bachelor's-degree transcript.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Candidates with a foreign qualification are assessed by the study-course Teaching Committee for equivalent knowledge; because the course is taught in Italian, those who graduated abroad must demonstrate Italian B2 proficiency (Italian language proficiency test; free University Italian courses if not passed). Non-EU students residing abroad enrol via the central international-enrolment route.

language_requirements.required_level: Italian B2 for instruction (required for foreign graduates), plus English B2 as a disciplinary competence (including disciplinary vocabulary).
accepted_proofs: English: an accepted B2 certificate, a bachelor's degree delivered in English, or passing the University's online B2 test / oral interview administered by the language-skills sector.
Italian: an accepted Italian-language certificate at B2, or passing the University's Italian language proficiency test (required for candidates who graduated abroad).
exemption_rules: English: automatically verified for candidates with a B2 certification or a bachelor's degree delivered in English.

Italian: candidates who graduated in Italy (Italian-taught degree) are not required to take the Italian test; those who fail the Italian test must take free Italian courses organised by the University.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English B2 is verified at the admission/assessment stage; Italian B2 is verified for foreign graduates at the assessment/enrolment stage because the programme is taught in Italian.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Verification of personal preparation (assessment of adequacy of personal readiness) - NOT a TOLC or competitive entrance exam. Public interview.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Candidates whose bachelor's final grade is below 92/110 (or below 9/10 of the maximum / below ECTS 'A'). Foreign graduates are also assessed for Italian and English language proficiency.
entry_exam_or_test.format: A public interview ascertaining general preparation, in particular knowledge of the fundamentals of civil and environmental engineering, with consideration of the bachelor's-degree transcript. Separate online language tests (English B2; Italian B2 for foreign graduates) may apply.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: [uncertain] The official requisites page currently publishes assessment-session dates for an earlier academic year (interviews on 6 and 26 September, 14 November, 16 January and 26 February). A.Y. 2026/2027-specific interview dates are not yet posted on the page; equivalent autumn-2026 / early-2027 sessions are expected.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Waived for bachelor's degrees with a final grade of at least 92/110 (or at least 9/10 of the maximum / ECTS 'A'). No published non-EU CGPA threshold (programme not on the English master's pre-evaluation list).

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
First-year master's pre-registration: from 9 April to 30 November 2026 (and until 10 March 2027 with late payment).
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Confirmation of enrolment from 15 July 2026 to 30 November 2026 (until 10 March 2027 with late payment); enrolment in years after the first runs 15 July to 5 October 2026 (arrears 6 October to 5 November 2026).
application_deadline_eu.notes: Open-access (non-ranked) programme; EU/resident applicants below the grade threshold must pass the personal-preparation verification before enrolling. First tuition installment approx. EUR 136 (EUR 16 stamp duty + EUR 120 regional tax) at enrolment; second installment by 10 December 2026; third by 23 June 2027.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.

This Italian-taught programme is NOT listed on the UniGeApply English master's pre-evaluation selection page; non-EU candidates apply via the general UniGeApply / central international-enrolment route and must satisfy the Italian-language requirement.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After UniGeApply eligibility, visa applicants complete the Universitaly pre-enrolment (from 4 May 2026) and then apply for the study visa at the Italian Embassy.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: None published for this programme (uses the standard fall-session close of 20 March 2026).
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: [uncertain] Because the course is taught in Italian, non-EU candidates must demonstrate Italian B2 and follow the standard international-enrolment route; the programme does not appear on the English master's pre-evaluation list, and no programme-specific non-EU CGPA threshold or early closure is published. The A.Y. 2026/27 fall window has now closed.

required_documents.document_name: Curriculum Vitae et Studiorum / bachelor's degree transcript
required_documents.notes: Considered by the Teaching Committee at the personal-preparation interview and to verify equivalent knowledge for foreign qualifications.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's / prior degree certificate (final or provisional)
required_documents.notes: Three-year, specialised or master's degree pursuant to DM 509/1999 or DM 270/2004, or equivalent.
Used to verify curricular CFU (36 CFU L-7 basic SSDs + 45 CFU Civil and Environmental/Territorial Engineering), unless directly admitted via L-7.

required_documents.document_name: English Language Proficiency Certificate (B2) or English-medium-of-instruction declaration
required_documents.notes: B2 certificate or bachelor's delivered in English; otherwise the University's online B2 test / oral interview.
Required for candidates who graduated abroad because the programme is taught in Italian; if the Italian test is failed, free University Italian courses must be taken.

required_documents.document_name: Legalisation or CIMEA Statement of Verification, plus Declaration of Value or CIMEA Statement of Comparability
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU visa applicants with a foreign qualification to complete enrolment, together with passport, transcript and degree certificate.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment submission and study-visa evidence
required_documents.notes: Visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment (from 4 May 2026) after UniGeApply eligibility, then apply for the study visa.

source_quotes.quote: CIVIL ENGINEERING, Course Code 11926, Degree Class LM-23 R (Civil Engineering), Teaching Language Italian, 120 CFU, 2 years, Genova (DICCA - Dipartimento di Ingegneria Civile, Chimica e Ambientale).

Minimum 36 CFU in basic training activities within scientific-disciplinary sectors of Class L-7 Civil and Environmental Engineering; Minimum 45 CFU in core training activities in Civil Engineering, Environmental and Territorial Engineering disciplines; Excellent knowledge of both oral and written English (level B2) with reference also to disciplinary vocabulary.
If you graduated abroad, you will have to take a proof of Italian language skills [at B2 level] ... If you fail the test, you must take free Italian courses organized by the University of Genoa.
You are exempt from verification if you earned a bachelor's degree with a final grade of at least 92/110 or equivalent classification 'A' under the ECTS system.
Civil Engineering does not appear in the list of English-taught master's degrees on the UniGeApply non-EU pre-evaluation selection page.

uncertainty_notes: Teaching language CONFIRMED as Italian: the corsi.unige.it catalogue lists Teaching Language Italian, the requisites page requires foreign graduates to pass an Italian B2 test (treating English B2 only as a disciplinary competence), and the programme does NOT appear on the UniGeApply English master's pre-evaluation list. This resolves the task's open question - the programme is Italian-taught (not English+Italian), with an English-competence requirement.
Programme is open-access (free access); the 'test' on the catalogue page is the assessment of adequacy of personal preparation, not a TOLC or competitive entrance exam.

Because it is Italian-taught, the standard non-EU CGPA thresholds and early closures used for the English masters do not apply; non-EU candidates follow the general international-enrolment route and must demonstrate Italian B2.
A.Y. 2026/2027-specific interview/test dates are not yet published on the requisites page (it shows an earlier academic year's sessions).
EU/spring deadlines reflect the institution-wide UniGeApply window plus the corsi.unige.it enrolment calendar; no programme-specific first-year EU application deadline is separately published.
